items.briefing:
A new report reveals that several ASX 200 companies have been compromised by infostealer malware, which quietly harvests login credentials and sensitive data from infected devices. This is a serious concern for any organisation with connections to larger listed companies. Separately, the Asia Pacific region — including Australia — has become the top target for financially motivated cyberattacks, meaning Australian businesses of all sizes face heightened risk. Small and medium businesses in particular are being urged not to treat cybersecurity as optional.

A scam toolkit called CypherLoc is also driving millions of browser-based attacks, often targeting everyday users through malicious websites. Interestingly, only one in four Australian farmers are reportedly considering cyber insurance, highlighting a broader gap in preparedness across industries.

**Practical tip:** Check whether your staff are reusing passwords across work and personal accounts — infostealer malware often succeeds because stolen credentials work on multiple platforms. A
